title: In solution digestion DRAFT
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"88efa","text":"Perform all steps at   .","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":21,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"a7e3v","text":"Prepare denaturation buffer freshly on the day of the experiment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"","unit":32}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"e3erf","text":"In this procedure all steps are done at    to reduce unwanted denaturization of amino acid side-chains by denaturing agents. Never heat your sample.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":125,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":40,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"crdhs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"","unit":32}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"eeaor","text":"Buffers:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5sb64","text":"Denaturation buffer: 8M urea in 50 mM ABC (ammonium bicarbonate)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9pqm9","text":"Digestion buffer: 50 mM ABC in water, pH 8. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9fgr7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5bc19","text":"Reagents: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3muhn","text":"DTT: 1 M  in 50 mM ABC.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"61a5s","text":"IAA: 1M in 50 mM ABC","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c8sat","text":"Formic acid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8311j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ns70","text":"Enzymes:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bnu96","text":"Lys-C: 1 µg/µL stock solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2ih45","text":"Trypsin: 1 µg/µL stock solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Make a working solution of DTT (100mM from 1M) Add DTT to a final concentration of 10 mM and incubate for 30 min at 22 °C. Shake at 1200 rpm.
Add 500 µL denaturation buffer to the cell pellet and resuspend thoroughly. If the pellet is not fully solubilised or remains highly viscous, increase the volume up to 1 mL total.
Make a working solution of IAA (100mM from 1M) Add IAA to a final concentration of 20 mM and incubate for 30 minutes at 22 °C in the dark. Shake at 1200 rpm.
No Lys-C added for initial optimisation. Incubate sample for 4 h at 22 °C with shaking at 1200 rpm
Dilute the sample by adding 4x volumes of digestion buffer to reduce urea concentration prior to trypsin digestion.

Resuspend trypsin vial in 20 µL 0.1% TFA and 80 µL ABC to generate a 0.2 µg/µL trypsin solution. Add 5 µL trypsin solution (~1 µg trypsin) per sample. Incubate overnight at 37°C. Incubate overnight at 37 °C
Add formic acid to a final concentration of 1% to quench digestion
Centrifuge briefly to pellet insoluble material and transfer the peptide-containing supernatant to a new tube
Proceed to load the sample onto equilibrated C18 StageTips (samples can stay in the fridge until the tips are equilibrated)
Disrupt the cells with vigorous pipetting
Centrifuge at high speed to clarify the lysate (5,000 × g, 22 °C, 5 min) and transfer the supernatant to a new tube
Centrifuge lysate at 16,000 rcf, 10 min, 4°C to pellet insoluble debris and transfer the supernatant to a new tube.

Determine protein concentration using the BCA assay. Prepare 1:10 and 1:20 lysate dilutions and use the dilution that falls within the reliable range of the BSA standard curve. Calculate the volume of lysate required for 30 µg protein input.
creator: Amber Minhas
